attributes.action: Make all CHARGED with Domestic Violence/Abuse sign a register.
attributes.additional_details:
Domestic Violence/Abuse is at an all time high. 

Crime survey for 2025 estimated that 3.8 million people aged 16 years+ experienced domestic abuse. 
Estimated 2.2 million females and 1.5 million males have experienced domestic violence/abuse in the past year with 53,919 charged by the CPS. 

Those 53,919 people are a danger to other people and future relationships.
Everyone deserves to know who they are dating or including in their lives or their children’s lives.
attributes.background:
Change needs to happen. 
Claire’s law is extremely hard to access, although great it’s not serving to its full potential.
I am proposing to Parliament that they introduce a register that must be signed by anyone convicted of Domestic Violence/Abuse.

rejection.details: You may wish to sign this petition, which calls for similar action: "Implement an easily accessible public register of convicted domestic abusers." https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/742873
